name: Nibiru Planet X
description: Nibiru, also known as Planet X, is a hypothetical planetary body claimed by some to exist beyond Neptune and to periodically pass through the inner solar system, causing catastrophic events on Earth. The theory's modern origins can be traced to Nancy Lieder, who claimed in 1995 that she was contacted by extraterrestrials who warned her of Nibiru's impending arrival in 2003, which would trigger a pole shift. This date was later revised. Proponents often cite interpretations of Sumerian mythology, particularly the works of Zecharia Sitchin, who posited that Nibiru is home to an alien race called the Anunnaki, who created humanity. They also point to alleged government cover-ups, unexplained astronomical anomalies, and increased seismic activity as evidence. Mainstream science dismisses Nibiru as a hoax and pseudoscience. Astronomers confirm there is no evidence of a large planet on a collision course with Earth or any other planet in the outer solar system behaving as described. The supposed Sumerian connections are also disputed by experts in Sumerian language and culture, who argue that Sitchin's interpretations are inaccurate and selective. Observations of planetary movements are well-documented, and the gravitational effects of a planet the size of Nibiru would have already been detected if it existed in the hypothesized orbit. Despite lacking scientific validity, the Nibiru Planet X theory persists due to its apocalyptic narrative and appeal to distrust in established institutions.
subtitle: Apocalyptic planetary collision conspiracy theory

claims.claim: Nibiru is a planet on a collision course with Earth.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of Sumerian texts, alleged government cover-ups, increased seismic activity.
claims.criticism: No scientific evidence supports the existence of Nibiru; Sumerian interpretations are disputed; seismic activity is naturally occurring.

claims.claim: Nibiru is inhabited by the Anunnaki, who created humanity.
claims.evidence: Zecharia Sitchin's interpretations of Sumerian mythology.
claims.criticism: Sitchin's translations and interpretations are considered inaccurate by scholars of Sumerian language and culture.

claims.claim: Governments are covering up the existence of Nibiru to prevent mass panic.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal claims, conspiracy theories, distrust of authority.
claims.criticism: The logistics of such a large-scale cover-up are implausible; no credible evidence has emerged despite numerous opportunities for leaks.

keyFigures.name: Nancy Lieder
keyFigures.role: Originator of the modern Nibiru cataclysm theory.

keyFigures.name: Zecharia Sitchin
keyFigures.role: Popularized the idea of Nibiru as a home planet of the Anunnaki.

keyFigures.name: Mike Brown
keyFigures.role: Astronomer who discovered Eris, contributing to the 'Planet X' discussion but debunking Nibiru claims.

timeline.event: Nancy Lieder begins promoting the Nibiru cataclysm theory.
Initial predicted date of Nibiru's arrival, which passed without incident.
Nibiru linked to the Mayan calendar apocalypse, further fueling the conspiracy.
